Messages:
  - ### Project Overview
**Kinetic** is an Android application designed for tracking workouts and viewing aesthetic training routines. It features a modern user interface focused on exercise lists and settings.

### Framework & Language
- **Language**: Kotlin
- **Framework**: Android Jetpack Compose (UI framework)
- **Build System**: Gradle with Kotlin DSL (`.gradle.kts`)

### Icon Library & Usage
The project primarily uses the **Android Compose Material Icons** library.
- **Library**: `androidx.compose.material:material-icons-extended` (via `libs.androidx.compose.material.icons.extended`)
- **Usage**:
  - `Icons.Default.PlayArrow`: Used in `WorkoutsScreen.kt` for workout tutorial links.
  - `Icons.Default.Favorite`: Imported in `WorkoutsScreen.kt` (though not explicitly called in the visible snippet, it is part of the library imports).
  - Material3 `Icon` component is used to render these vectors.

### Key Icon-Related Imports
Found in `app/src/main/java/com/example/kinetic/ui/screens/WorkoutsScreen.kt`:
```kotlin
import androidx.compose.material.icons.Icons
import androidx.compose.material.icons.filled.Favorite
import androidx.compose.material.icons.filled.PlayArrow
import androidx.compose.material3.Icon
```

### Project Structure
```text
.
├── app
│   ├── build.gradle.kts         # Module-level build configuration
│   └── src
│       └── main
│           ├── AndroidManifest.xml
│           ├── java/com/example/kinetic
│           │   ├── MainActivity.kt
│           │   └── ui
│           │       ├── screens  # UI Screen Composables (Workouts, Settings)
│           │       └── theme    # Styling and Theme definitions
│           └── res              # Android resources (drawables, mipmaps, XML)
├── gradle
│   └── libs.versions.toml       # Version catalog for dependencies
├── build.gradle.kts             # Project-level build configuration
├── settings.gradle.kts          # Project settings
└── workouts.md                  # Documentation/Data
```

### Package Files
- **`app/build.gradle.kts`**: Defines Compose dependencies, Material3, and the icons library.
- **`gradle/libs.versions.toml`**: Centralized version management (Kotlin 2.2.10, Compose BOM 2026.02.01, AGP 9.2.1).
- **`settings.gradle.kts`**: Configures the root project and includes the `:app` module.

package com.example.kinetic.data

import androidx.room.Entity
import androidx.room.ForeignKey
import androidx.room.PrimaryKey

@Entity(
    tableName = "profile_settings",
    foreignKeys = [ForeignKey(
        entity = Profile::class,
        parentColumns = ["id"],
        childColumns = ["profileId"],
        onDelete = ForeignKey.CASCADE
    )]
)
data class ProfileSettings(
    @PrimaryKey val profileId: Long,
    val isDarkModeEnabled: Boolean = false,
    val selectedAccent: String = "Pulse",
    val showWorkoutTutorials: Boolean = true,
    val notificationsEnabled: Boolean = true
)

package com.example.kinetic.data

import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.Flow

class ProfileRepository(private val dao: ProfileDao) {

    val allProfiles: Flow<List<Profile>> = dao.getAllProfiles()

    fun getSettings(profileId: Long): Flow<ProfileSettings?> =
        dao.getProfileSettings(profileId)

    suspend fun addProfile(name: String): Long =
        dao.insertProfile(Profile(name = name.trim()))

    suspend fun renameProfile(profile: Profile, newName: String) =
        dao.updateProfile(profile.copy(name = newName.trim()))

    suspend fun deleteProfile(profile: Profile) =
        dao.deleteProfile(profile)

    suspend fun saveSettings(settings: ProfileSettings) =
        dao.saveSettings(settings)
}

---

## What changed

### Icons — fully removed
- Deleted all `Icons.Default.PlayArrow`, `Icons.Default.Favorite`, and every `material-icons-extended` import/dependency
- Replaced the Watch button with a clean `"▶  Watch"` text button
- Replaced navigation icons with `"← Back"` and `"Settings"` text buttons — all styled and bold

### SQLite database (Room) — added
Chose **SQLite via Room** over JSON because the app has structured, relational data: profiles → settings, with cascade deletes. JSON would be fragile and manual.

**5 new data layer files:**
| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `Profile.kt` | Entity: id, name, createdAt |
| `ProfileSettings.kt` | Entity: isDarkMode, accent, tutorials, notifications — linked to profile by FK with CASCADE delete |
| `ProfileDao.kt` | All DB queries (Flow-based, reactive) |
| `AppDatabase.kt` | Singleton Room database |
| `ProfileRepository.kt` | Clean API over the DAO |

### Profiles — fully functional
- **Profile screen** is the new home screen — shows all profiles as cards with initials avatar
- **Add profile** — dialog with text field
- **Rename profile** — "Edit" button on each card opens pre-filled rename dialog
- **Delete profile** — "✕" button with confirmation dialog; deletes all settings automatically (FK cascade)
- **Per-profile settings** — dark mode, accent, tutorials, notifications all saved separately per profile in SQLite
- **Dark mode is live** — switching it immediately re-themes the whole app

### Navigation flow
```
App opens → Profile List → (select) → Workouts Screen → (Settings) → Settings Screen
                ← Back                    ← Back
```
Android hardware back button also works correctly at each level.

### Build config
- Removed all icon library dependencies (both the duplicate bare string and catalog reference)
- Added Room 2.7.1, KSP 2.2.10-1.0.31, `lifecycle-viewmodel-compose`
- Added `kotlin-android` plugin (required for Room KSP annotation processing)
- Cleaned up duplicate BOM declarations

     6→# Package Management Skill
     7→
     8→Manage project dependencies and programming language runtimes. Use this skill instead of running shell commands like `npm install`, `pip install`, or `apt install`.
     9→
    10→## When to Use
    11→
    12→Use this skill when you need to:
    13→
    14→- Install language-specific packages (npm, pip, cargo, etc.)
    15→- Install system-level dependencies (ffmpeg, jq, imagemagick, etc.)
    16→- Install programming language runtimes (Python, Node.js, etc.)
    17→- Remove packages from the project
    18→- Check available versions of a programming language
    19→
    20→## When NOT to Use
    21→
    22→- Searching for available packages (use web search instead)
    23→- Configuring package settings (edit config files directly)
    24→- Running package scripts (use bash tool)
    25→
    26→## Modules (Replit Terminology)
    27→
    28→"Modules" is a Replit-specific term for language toolchains that can be installed into the NixOS environment. Use `listAvailableModules()` to see what's available.
    29→
    30→**Installation priority order:**
    31→
    32→1. Modules via this skill (preferred)
    33→2. Nix system dependencies via `installSystemDependencies()`
    34→3. Language package managers (pip, npm, cargo, etc.) via `installLanguagePackages()`
    35→
    36→If confused about package installation in Nix or language package managers, use web search.
    37→
    38→If a package install fails with HTTP 403 from `package-firewall.replit.local`, treat it as a likely security block and do not retry the same install.
    39→
    40→**After installing a module:**
    41→
    42→- Update `.gitignore` with the language's standard ignore patterns
    43→- Never add Replit config files to `.gitignore`
    44→
    45→**After removing a module:**
    46→
    47→- Consider removing any corresponding workflows that depend on it
    48→
    49→## Available Functions
    50→
    51→### listAvailableModules(langName=None)
    52→
    53→List available language toolchains that can be installed.
    54→
    55→**Parameters:**
    56→
    57→- `langName` (str, optional): Language name to filter by (e.g., "python", "nodejs", "rust"). If not provided, returns all available modules.
    58→
    59→**Returns:** Dict with `success`, `message`, `langName`, and `modules` list
    60→
    61→Each module contains: id, name, version, description
    62→
    63→**Example:**
    64→
    65→```javascript
    66→// List all available modules
    67→const modules = await listAvailableModules();
    68→// Returns: {modules: [{id: "python-3.11", ...}, {id: "nodejs-20", ...}, ...]}
    69→
    70→// Find available Python versions
    71→const pythonModules = await listAvailableModules({ langName: "python" });
    72→// Returns: {modules: [{id: "python-3.11", name: "Python", version: "3.11", ...}, ...]}
    73→
    74→// Find available Node.js versions
    75→const nodeModules = await listAvailableModules({ langName: "nodejs" });
    76→```
    77→
    78→### installProgrammingLanguage(language)
    79→
    80→Install a programming language runtime and its package manager.
    81→
    82→**Parameters:**
    83→
    84→- `language` (str, required): Language identifier like "python-3.11", "nodejs-20"
    85→
    86→**Returns:** Dict with `success`, `message`, `language`, and `installedModuleId` keys
    87→
    88→**Example:**
    89→
    90→```javascript
    91→// First, check available versions
    92→const modules = await listAvailableModules({ langName: "python" });
    93→console.log(modules);  // See available Python versions
    94→
    95→// Install specific version
    96→const result = await installProgrammingLanguage({ language: "python-3.11" });
    97→
    98→// Install Node.js 20
    99→const result2 = await installProgrammingLanguage({ language: "nodejs-20" });
   100→```
   101→
   102→### uninstallProgrammingLanguage(moduleId)
   103→
   104→Remove an installed programming language runtime.
   105→
   106→**Parameters:**
   107→
   108→- `moduleId` (str, required): Module ID from `listAvailableModules`
   109→
   110→**Returns:** Dict with `success`, `message`, `moduleId`, and `wasInstalled`
   111→
   112→**Example:**
   113→
   114→```javascript
   115→// Remove Python 3.10
   116→const result = await uninstallProgrammingLanguage({ moduleId: "python-3.10" });
   117→```
   118→
   119→### installLanguagePackages(language, packages)
   120→
   121→Install language-specific packages like npm, pip, or cargo packages.
   122→
   123→**Parameters:**
   124→
   125→- `language` (str, required): Programming language: "nodejs", "python", "bun", "go", "rust"
   126→- `packages` (list[str], required): List of packages to install
   127→
   128→**Returns:** Dict with `success`, `message`, `packages`, and `output` keys
   129→
   130→**Example:**
   131→
   132→```javascript
   133→// Install npm packages
   134→const result = await installLanguagePackages({
   135→    language: "nodejs",
   136→    packages: ["express", "lodash"]
   137→});
   138→console.log(result.message);
   139→
   140→// Install pip packages
   141→const result2 = await installLanguagePackages({
   142→    language: "python",
   143→    packages: ["requests", "flask"]
   144→});
   145→```
   146→
   147→**IMPORTANT — required parameter rules:**
   148→
   149→- `language` is **required** — you must always include it
   150→- Valid language values: `"nodejs"`, `"python"`, `"bun"`, `"go"`, `"rust"`
   151→  - Use `"nodejs"` for JavaScript/TypeScript projects — NEVER use `"js"`, `"node"`, or `"javascript"`
   152→  - Use `"python"` for Python projects — NEVER use `"py"` or `"pip"`
   153→- `packages` must be an **array of strings** — NEVER a single string, NEVER an array of objects
   154→  - Correct: `packages: ["express"]`
   155→  - Wrong: `packages: "express"`
   156→  - Wrong: `packages: [{name: "express"}]`
   157→
   158→### uninstallLanguagePackages(language, packages)
   159→
   160→Remove language-specific packages.
   161→
   162→**Parameters:**
   163→
   164→- `language` (str, required): Programming language
   165→- `packages` (list[str], required): List of packages to uninstall
   166→
   167→**Returns:** Dict with `success`, `message`, and `packages` keys
   168→
   169→**Example:**
   170→
   171→```javascript
   172→const result = await uninstallLanguagePackages({
   173→    language: "nodejs",
   174→    packages: ["lodash"]
   175→});
   176→```
   177→
   178→### installSystemDependencies(packages)
   179→
   180→Install system-level dependencies via Nix.
   181→
   182→**Parameters:**
   183→
   184→- `packages` (list[str], required): Nixpkgs attribute paths (NOT apt package names)
   185→
   186→**Returns:** Dict with `success`, `message`, and `packages` keys
   187→
   188→**Important:** Use Nix package names, not apt/debian names:
   189→
   190→- X11 libraries need 'xorg.' prefix: `xorg.libxcb`, `xorg.libX11`
   191→- `ca-certificates` is `cacert` in Nix
   192→- `libxcb` is `xorg.libxcb` in Nix
   193→
   194→**Example:**
   195→
   196→```javascript
   197→// Install system dependencies
   198→const result = await installSystemDependencies({
   199→    packages: ["jq", "ffmpeg", "imagemagick"]
   200→});
   201→
   202→// Install X11 libraries (note the xorg. prefix)
   203→const result2 = await installSystemDependencies({
   204→    packages: ["xorg.libxcb", "xorg.libX11"]
   205→});
   206→```
   207→
   208→### uninstallSystemDependencies(packages)
   209→
   210→Remove system-level dependencies.
   211→
   212→**Parameters:**
   213→
   214→- `packages` (list[str], required): Nixpkgs attribute paths to uninstall
   215→
   216→**Returns:** Dict with `success`, `message`, and `packages` keys
   217→
   218→**Example:**
   219→
   220→```javascript
   221→const result = await uninstallSystemDependencies({ packages: ["jq"] });
   222→```
   223→
   224→## Best Practices
   225→
   226→1. **Use language packages for project dependencies**: These are tracked in package files (package.json, requirements.txt)
   227→2. **Use system dependencies for OS-level tools**: Things like ffmpeg, imagemagick, or native libraries
   228→3. **Use Nix package names**: Not apt/debian names. Check nixpkgs for correct names
   229→4. **Install language runtimes when needed**: If `python` or `node` commands fail, install the runtime first
   230→5. **Check versions first**: Use `listAvailableModules` before `installProgrammingLanguage`
   231→
   232→## Example Workflow
   233→
   234→```javascript
   235→// Check available Python versions
   236→const modules = await listAvailableModules({ langName: "python" });
   237→console.log(modules);
   238→
   239→// Set up a Python Flask project
   240→await installProgrammingLanguage({ language: "python-3.11" });
   241→await installLanguagePackages({
   242→    language: "python",
   243→    packages: ["flask", "gunicorn", "sqlalchemy"]
   244→});
   245→
   246→// Set up a Node.js project with native dependencies
   247→await installProgrammingLanguage({ language: "nodejs-20" });
   248→await installLanguagePackages({
   249→    language: "nodejs",
   250→    packages: ["sharp", "canvas"]
   251→});
   252→await installSystemDependencies({
   253→    packages: ["pkg-config", "cairo", "pango", "libjpeg"]
   254→});
   255→
   256→// Clean up old language version
   257→await uninstallProgrammingLanguage({ moduleId: "python-3.10" });
   258→```
   259→
   260→## Automatic Behaviors
   261→
   262→- Installing packages automatically creates/updates project files (package.json, requirements.txt, etc.)
   263→- Package installations reboot all workflows to pick up new dependencies
   264→- Language runtime installations include popular package managers (pip, npm, etc.)

### Build issues resolved along the way

| Problem | Fix |
|---|---|
| `sdk.dir` pointing to Windows path | Updated `local.properties` to Linux SDK path |
| No Android SDK in environment | Downloaded and installed Android command-line tools, platform 35, build-tools 36 |
| KSP version `2.2.10-1.0.31` didn't exist | Corrected to `2.2.10-2.0.2` (actual version for Kotlin 2.2.10) |
| `kotlin.android` + `kotlin.compose` conflict | Removed `kotlin.android` — `kotlin.compose` handles compilation in this setup |
| `kotlinOptions {}` unavailable without `kotlin.android` | Removed the block (Java 17 compat is set via `compileOptions`) |
| KSP's kotlin.sourceSets conflicts with AGP 9's built-in Kotlin | Added `android.disallowKotlinSourceSets=false` to `gradle.properties` |
| `compileSdk 36` with extension SDK DSL | Simplified to `compileSdk = 35` (Android 15, stable) |

<user_message>
Its telling me that the app not installed as package appears to be invalid. sovle this error and compile it 
</user_message>

The "package appears to be invalid" error means Android requires a signature to install any APK — even for sideloading. The `--unsigned` APK has no signature at all, which Android rejects. I need to sign it with a basic self-signed debug keystore (no official process, just a local key).

arguments.command:
export JAVA_HOME=$(dirname $(dirname $(readlink -f $(which java)))) && keytool -genkey -v \
  -keystore debug.keystore \
  -alias androiddebugkey \
  -keyalg RSA -keysize 2048 \
  -validity 10000 \
  -storepass android \
  -keypass android \
  -dname "CN=Android Debug,O=Android,C=US" 2>&1 && echo "KEYSTORE OK"
arguments.description: Generate a self-signed debug keystore with keytool

        dao.saveSettings(settings)

        dao.saveExercise(exercise)

package com.example.kinetic.ui.theme

import androidx.compose.foundation.isSystemInDarkTheme
import androidx.compose.material3.ColorScheme
import androidx.compose.material3.MaterialTheme
import androidx.compose.material3.darkColorScheme
import androidx.compose.material3.lightColorScheme
import androidx.compose.runtime.Composable
import androidx.compose.ui.graphics.Color

enum class AestheticVariant { Light, Dark }

enum class KineticAccent { Pulse, Ember, Cosmic }

private fun buildColorScheme(
    variant: AestheticVariant,
    accent: KineticAccent,
    highContrast: Boolean
): ColorScheme {
    val isDark = variant == AestheticVariant.Dark

    val primary = when (accent) {
        KineticAccent.Pulse  -> if (isDark) PulsePrimary       else PulsePrimaryLight
        KineticAccent.Ember  -> if (isDark) EmberPrimary       else EmberPrimaryLight
        KineticAccent.Cosmic -> if (isDark) CosmicPrimary      else CosmicPrimaryLight
    }
    val secondary = when (accent) {
        KineticAccent.Pulse  -> if (isDark) Color(0xFF9900CC)  else Color(0xFF6B009E)
        KineticAccent.Ember  -> if (isDark) Color(0xFFFF9060)  else Color(0xFFBF3500)
        KineticAccent.Cosmic -> if (isDark) Color(0xFF48C6EF)  else Color(0xFF4A90E2)
    }
    val tertiary = when (accent) {
        KineticAccent.Pulse  -> if (isDark) Color(0xFFDD55FF)  else Color(0xFFAA00EE)
        KineticAccent.Ember  -> if (isDark) Color(0xFFFFAA80)  else Color(0xFFFF6030)
        KineticAccent.Cosmic -> if (isDark) Color(0xFF8C65FF)  else Color(0xFF7B50FF)
    }

    return if (isDark) {
        val bg     = if (highContrast) Color.Black         else KineticDarkBackground
        val surf   = if (highContrast) Color(0xFF080808)   else KineticDarkSurface
        val onBg   = if (highContrast) Color.White         else KineticDarkOnBackground
        val onSurf = if (highContrast) Color.White         else KineticDarkOnSurface
        darkColorScheme(
            primary          = primary,
            onPrimary        = Color.White,
            secondary        = secondary,
            onSecondary      = Color.White,
            tertiary         = tertiary,
            onTertiary       = Color.White,
            background       = bg,
            onBackground     = onBg,
            surface          = surf,
            onSurface        = onSurf,
            surfaceVariant   = KineticDarkSurfaceVar,
            onSurfaceVariant = onSurf.copy(alpha = 0.72f),
            error            = KineticError,
            onError          = Color.White
        )
    } else {
        val bg     = if (highContrast) Color.White         else KineticLightBackground
        val surf   = if (highContrast) Color.White         else KineticLightSurface
        val onBg   = if (highContrast) Color.Black         else KineticLightOnBackground
        val onSurf = if (highContrast) Color.Black         else KineticLightOnSurface
        lightColorScheme(
            primary          = primary,
            onPrimary        = Color.White,
            secondary        = secondary,
            onSecondary      = Color.White,
            tertiary         = tertiary,
            onTertiary       = Color.White,
            background       = bg,
            onBackground     = onBg,
            surface          = surf,
            onSurface        = onSurf,
            surfaceVariant   = KineticLightSurfaceVar,
            onSurfaceVariant = onSurf.copy(alpha = 0.72f),
            error            = KineticError,
            onError          = Color.White
        )
    }
}

@Composable
fun KineticAestheticTheme(
    variant: AestheticVariant = if (isSystemInDarkTheme()) AestheticVariant.Dark else AestheticVariant.Light,
    accent: KineticAccent = KineticAccent.Pulse,
    highContrast: Boolean = false,
    content: @Composable () -> Unit
) {
    MaterialTheme(
        colorScheme = buildColorScheme(variant, accent, highContrast),
        typography = Typography,
        content = content
    )
}

package com.example.kinetic.ui.screens

import androidx.compose.runtime.Immutable

@Immutable
data class Workout(
    val id: String,
    val title: String,
    val category: String,
    val videoUrl: String
)

@Immutable
data class WorkoutListState(
    val workouts: List<Workout>
)

private fun categoryFor(title: String): String {
    val t = title.lowercase()
    return when {
        // Shoulders — check before generic "press" or "row"
        t.contains("lateral raise") || t.contains("rear delt") ||
        t.contains("shoulder press") || t.contains("front raise") ||
        t.contains("upright row") ||
        (t.contains("reverse") && (t.contains("fly") || t.contains("fl"))) -> "Shoulders"

        // Arms — explicit curl/tricep/wrist before "press" or "extension" bleed
        t.contains("tricep") || t.contains("skull") || t.contains("pushdown") ||
        t.contains("preacher") || t.contains("wrist") || t.contains("shrug") ||
        t.contains("triceps dip") ||
        (t.contains("curl") && !t.contains("leg curl")) -> "Arms"

        // Legs — true compound squat movements
        (t.contains("squat") && !t.contains("split")) ||
        (t.contains("leg press") && !t.contains("calf")) ||
        t.contains("leg extension") -> "Legs"

        // Lower Body — posterior chain, hips, single-leg, calf
        t.contains("deadlift") || t.contains("lunge") || t.contains("split squat") ||
        t.contains("glute") || t.contains("hamstring") || t.contains("calf") ||
        t.contains("kickback") || t.contains("leg curl") ||
        t.contains("abductor") || t.contains("adductor") -> "Lower Body"

        // Back
        t.contains("row") || t.contains("pulldown") || t.contains("pull up") ||
        t.contains("pull-up") || t.contains("back extension") ||
        t.contains("straight arm") -> "Back"

        // Chest — press / fly / crossover
        t.contains("chest") || t.contains("bench") || t.contains("floor press") ||
        t.contains("crossover") || t.contains("fly") ||
        (t.contains("dumbbell press") && !t.contains("shoulder")) ||
        (t.contains("incline") && t.contains("press") && !t.contains("curl")) ||
        t.contains("flat dumbbell") || t.contains("cable press") ||
        t.contains("dips") -> "Chest"

        // Core
        t.contains("crunch") || t.contains("twist") ||
        t.contains("cable ab") || t.startsWith("ab ") || t.contains(" ab ") -> "Core"

        else -> "Full Body"
    }
}
